rationale: The citing paper discusses Park and Bradlow (2005) in its core literature review and Table 1 as a key prior bid-level auction study that models bidding behavior dimensions such as whether, when, and how much to bid. It then uses that prior stream to frame a gap: existing models (including this one) focus on price-based settings without bidder differentiation in value to the buyer. This contrast helps motivate the citing paper's conceptual setup and claimed contribution in buyer-determined reverse procurement auctions. The citation is therefore more than a passing mention, but it is not a direct method transfer.
relation_description: The citing paper uses the cited paper as a meaningful prior benchmark in auction bidding modeling and positions its own contribution as addressing limitations of that prior stream in differentiated, buyer-determined procurement settings.
